content: Session started. Task: Analyze SPEC.md for Rust cross-platform prediction market arbitrage system and build it. Spec covers 8 crates (arb-types, arb-polymarket, arb-kalshi, arb-matcher, arb-engine, arb-risk, arb-db, arb-cli) with SQLite, TUI, risk management. Launching Planning, Engineering, and Validation teams in parallel.
Delegated to Architect: Analyze SPEC.md and produce architecture-plan.md with three sections — architecture analysis, phased build plan, and spec gaps.

Produced architecture-plan.md with: (1) Architecture analysis — validated 8-crate decomposition, dependency graph, data flow, connector trait, concurrency model, SQLite choice. Key concern: arb-engine is fat-middle crate but acceptable for MVP. (2) Phased build plan — 5 phases from foundation to TUI. Phase 1 fully detailed with file lists, type definitions, error hierarchies, test criteria. (3) Spec gaps — identified 15 gaps (4 critical/blocker, 4 high, 7 medium/low) plus risk register with 7 risks. Top blockers: undefined types (OrderBook, PriceUpdate, etc.), missing error hierarchy, no WebSocket reconnection strategy, missing clap dependency.

Delegated to Security Reviewer: Full security audit of SPEC.md covering 7 areas — secret management, crypto signing, financial risk, network security, data security, dependencies, operational security.

Security review complete. 22 findings: 1 CRITICAL (dual-order race condition), 6 HIGH (plain-text keys, no nonce strategy, no kill switch, stale price data, no access controls), 10 MEDIUM, 4 LOW, 1 INFO. Top recommendations: cancel-on-failure pattern for orders, encrypted keystores, emergency stop mechanism, stale data protection, cargo audit.

Delegated to Backend Dev: Build Phase 1 Foundation — workspace setup, arb-types (all domain types), arb-db (SQLite schema + repo), arb-risk (skeleton), arb-cli (skeleton), stub crates for remaining 4.

Phase 1 Foundation complete. 26 files created across 8 crates. cargo check --workspace passes (5 dead_code warnings for CLI config fields consumed later). All domain types from Section 4 implemented, SQL schema matches Section 5.1, config matches Section 11.1, PredictionMarketConnector trait from Section 6.1 defined. Key decisions: runtime sqlx queries (no DATABASE_URL needed at compile time), include_str! for migrations, concrete ArbError in trait.

All 3 teams completed. Planning: architecture plan with 15 spec gaps. Engineering: Phase 1 foundation compiles (26 files, 8 crates). Validation: 22 security findings (1 critical). Build verified: cargo check --workspace passes. Ready for Phase 2 (platform connectors).
